title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our classic recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a homemade feast. Get the recipe!
h2Headings: Ingredients You'll Need for Butter Chicken
Step-by-Step Instructions for Classic Murgh Makhani
Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant bowl of classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with cream and cilantro, served with naan.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani), a beloved Indian dish perfect for any occasion.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a beloved Indian classic featuring tender chicken simmered in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. Its signature mild spice and velvety texture come from a blend of aromatic spices, butter, and cream. This iconic dish offers a delightful balance of sweet, tangy, and savory flavors, making it a universal favorite.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 mins

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, turmeric powder, red chili powder, garam masala, and 1 tsp salt. Mix well to coat all chicken pieces. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary, to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 5-7 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned and cooked through. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 2 tbsp butter. Once melted, add the chopped onion and saute until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and green chilies (if using), cooking for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the cumin powder and coriander powder, cooking for 30 seconds.

instructions.title: Simmer and Blend the Gravy
instructions.text: Add the crushed tomatoes and soaked cashews (drained) to the pan. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have softened and the oil starts to separate. Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. Strain the gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan for an extra smooth texture.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Gravy
instructions.text: Return the pan with the strained gravy to medium heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces to the gravy. Stir well to coat the chicken. Simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the sauce.

instructions.title: Finish the Butter Chicken
instructions.text: Stir in the kasoori methi (crushed between your palms), sugar, and the remaining 1 tbsp butter. Cook for 2-3 minutes until the butter has melted and is fully incorporated. Finally, gently stir in the heavy cream. Heat through for another 1-2 minutes, but do not boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust salt or sugar if needed.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with naan bread, roti, or basmati rice. Enjoy your homemade Classic Butter Chicken!

tips: For an authentic smoky flavor, you can char the marinated chicken slightly over an open flame or under a broiler before adding it to the gravy.
To enhance the aroma of kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), lightly toast it in a dry pan for a minute before crushing and adding to the gravy.
For an extra rich and velvety texture, ensure you blend the gravy base until perfectly smooth and strain it through a fine-mesh sieve.
article:
Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani: A Culinary Journey Through India

Butter Chicken, known in its native tongue as Murgh Makhani, stands as a towering giant in the world of Indian cuisine. Revered globally for its rich, creamy texture and tantalizingly balanced flavors, it's a dish that has charmed palates from Delhi to New York, London to Sydney. More than just a meal, it's an experience – a symphony of tender chicken enveloped in a velvety tomato and butter-based sauce, fragrant with aromatic spices. If you've ever yearned to recreate that restaurant-quality magic in your own kitchen, you've come to the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the history, ingredients, and precise steps to master the art of classic Murgh Makhani.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, translates literally to "chicken with butter," a name that perfectly encapsulates its defining ingredient. Its origins trace back to the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi, India, in the 1950s. Legend has it that the chefs, needing to utilize leftover Tandoori chicken, simmered it in a rich tomato gravy enriched with butter and cream. The result was an instant sensation, quickly becoming a staple of Indian fine dining and eventually a global culinary phenomenon.

At its heart, Butter Chicken is a harmonious blend of contrasting elements. The chicken, traditionally marinated in yogurt and spices, is often cooked in a tandoor (clay oven) or pan-fried until charred and tender. This flavorful chicken is then introduced to a lusciously smooth sauce made from tomatoes, butter, cream, and a specific blend of mild spices like fenugreek (kasoori methi), garam masala, and cardamom. The sauce's signature sweetness, often balanced with a touch of sugar, cuts through the acidity of the tomatoes and the richness of the dairy, creating a uniquely comforting and unforgettable taste profile. It's a testament to the ingenuity of Indian cooking, transforming simple ingredients into a dish that is both elegant and deeply satisfying.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Butter Chicken

Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken requires a thoughtful selection of fresh ingredients and a well-stocked spice cabinet. Don't be intimidated by the list; each component plays a crucial role in building the dish's iconic flavor profile.

**For the Chicken Marinade:**
*   **Chicken:** 1.5 lbs (approximately 680g) boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces. Thighs are recommended for juiciness.
*   **Plain Yogurt:** 1/2 cup (thick, unsweetened)
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 1 tablespoon
*   **Kashmiri Red Chili Powder:** 1 teaspoon (for color, mild heat)
*   **Turmeric Powder:** 1/2 teaspoon
*   **Garam Masala:** 1/2 teaspoon
*   **Salt:** 1/2 teaspoon

**For the Makhani Sauce:**
*   **Unsalted Butter:** 4 tablespoons (divided)
*   **Oil:** 1 tablespoon (vegetable or canola)
*   **Onion:** 1 medium, finely chopped or pureed
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 1 tablespoon
*   **Tomato Puree/Crushed Tomatoes:** 1 (28-ounce) can, or 4-5 large fresh tomatoes pureed
*   **Cashews:** 1/4 cup, soaked in warm water for 30 minutes (for creaminess and body)
*   **Spices:**
    *   Cumin Powder: 1 teaspoon
    *   Coriander Powder: 1 teaspoon
    *   Kashmiri Red Chili Powder: 1 teaspoon
    *   Garam Masala: 1 teaspoon
    *   Fenugreek Leaves (Kasoori Methi): 1 tablespoon, crushed between palms
*   **Sugar:** 1-2 teaspoons (to balance acidity)
*   **Heavy Cream:** 1/2 cup (or half-and-half)
*   **Salt:** To taste
*   **Fresh Cilantro:** For garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Classic Murgh Makhani

Follow these detailed steps to create a truly unforgettable Butter Chicken experience at home.

**Step 1: Marinate the Chicken**
In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients (yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, salt). Mix well, ensuring each piece is coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor.

**Step 2: Cook the Marinated Chicken**
Heat 1 tablespoon of butter and 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan (cook in batches if necessary). Cook for 3-5 minutes per side, until the chicken is browned and cooked through with slight charring. This browning adds depth of flavor. Remove the cooked chicken from the pan and set aside.

**Step 3: Prepare the Sauce Base**
In the same pan, reduce heat to medium and add the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped or pureed onion and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

**Step 4: Build the Tomato & Spice Foundation**
Stir in the cumin powder, coriander powder, and Kashmiri red chili powder. Cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly, until aromatic. Pour in the tomato puree or crushed tomatoes. Add the soaked and drained cashews to the sauce. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil separates slightly at the edges. This allows the tomatoes to cook down and sweeten.

**Step 5: Achieve Silky Smoothness**
Remove the pan from heat. Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra luxurious texture, you can pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any remaining solids. Return the smooth sauce to the pan.

**Step 6: Finish the Makhani Sauce**
Place the pan back on low heat. Stir in the garam masala, crushed kasoori methi, and sugar. Add the cooked chicken pieces to the sauce. Pour in the heavy cream and stir gently to combine. Simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the rich sauce. Adjust salt and sugar to taste.

**Step 7: Garnish and Serve**
Garnish your classic Butter Chicken with fresh chopped cilantro and an optional swirl of extra cream or a small pat of butter.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

Achieving that truly exceptional restaurant-style Butter Chicken isn't just about following the steps; a few expert tips can elevate your dish from good to extraordinary.

*   **Chicken Choice is Key:** While breast meat works, boneless, skinless chicken thighs are highly recommended. Their higher fat content keeps them incredibly juicy and tender even after cooking, preventing them from drying out in the rich sauce.
*   **Marination Time:** Don't skimp on marination. The longer the chicken marinates, the more flavor it absorbs, resulting in more tender and delicious chicken pieces.
*   **The Power of Kasoori Methi:** Fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) are non-negotiable for authentic Butter Chicken. They impart a distinct, slightly bitter, and sweet aroma that is crucial to the dish's unique profile. Gently toast them in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing them between your palms to release their full fragrance.
*   **Blend, Blend, Blend (and Strain!):** The hallmark of a classic Murgh Makhani is its incredibly smooth, velvety sauce. Use a high-speed blender and blend until there are absolutely no visible solids. For the ultimate restaurant-quality texture, pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve. This extra step ensures a truly luxurious mouthfeel.
*   **Balance is Everything:** Taste your sauce frequently. The sweetness from the sugar and the richness of the cream should perfectly balance the tanginess of the tomatoes and the warmth of the spices. Don't be afraid to adjust these elements to suit your preference. A touch more sugar can cut through acidity, while a pinch more salt can brighten all the flavors.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments, transforming it into a complete and satisfying meal.

*   **Breads:** The quintessential pairing is with warm, fluffy **Naan bread**, perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich sauce. Other excellent choices include Tandoori Roti, Laccha Paratha, or even simple Chapati.
*   **Rice:** For a heartier meal, serve it alongside fragrant **Basmati rice** or a subtly flavored Jeera Rice (cumin rice). The plain rice acts as a wonderful canvas for the complex flavors of the Butter Chicken.
*   **Cooling Sides:** To balance the richness, offer a refreshing **Cucumber Raita** (yogurt with grated cucumber and spices) or a simple green salad with a light vinaigrette.
*   **Pickles and Chutneys:** A small dollop of mango pickle or a sweet tamarind chutney can add an exciting burst of flavor and texture.
*   **Beverages:** Pair with a traditional sweet or salty Lassi, a light Indian beer, or even a crisp white wine like a Sauvignon Blanc or Pinot Grigio, which can cut through the richness.

## Variations and Frequently Asked Questions

While the classic Murgh Makhani is a masterpiece, there are many ways to adapt it and common questions that arise.

**Variations:**
*   **Healthier Options:** For a lighter version, reduce the amount of butter and cream, substituting some with Greek yogurt or cashew cream. You can also grill or bake the chicken instead of pan-frying.
*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** The sauce base is incredibly versatile. Replace chicken with paneer (Indian cheese) for Paneer Makhani, or use firm tofu, chickpeas, or mixed vegetables for delicious plant-based alternatives.
*   **Spicier Butter Chicken:** While traditionally mild, you can increase the amount of Kashmiri red chili powder or add a touch of regular red chili powder for more heat. A finely chopped green chili added with the ginger-garlic paste also works.
*   **Smoked Butter Chicken:** For an authentic touch reminiscent of a tandoor, you can give your cooked chicken a smoky flavor. Place a small piece of burning charcoal in a small foil bowl within the pot of sauce, drizzle a teaspoon of ghee over it, and quickly cover the pot for 5-7 minutes.

**Frequently Asked Questions:**

*   **Can I make Butter Chicken ahead of time?** Absolutely! Butter Chicken often tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to meld and deepen. Store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
*   **Does Butter Chicken freeze well?** Yes, it freezes exceptionally well. Allow it to cool completely, then transfer to freezer-safe containers. It can be frozen for up to 2-3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator and reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of water or cream if needed to adjust consistency.
*   **What if I don't have cashews?** While cashews contribute to the sauce's signature creaminess, you can substitute them with blanched almonds (soaked and peeled) or omit them for a slightly thinner sauce. Some recipes use a small amount of poppy seeds for thickening as well.
*   **Why is my sauce not smooth?** The most common reason is insufficient blending. Ensure you blend the sauce for several minutes until it's completely smooth, and consider straining it through a fine-mesh sieve for optimal silkiness.
*   **Is Butter Chicken spicy?** Traditionally, classic Butter Chicken is mild to moderately spicy, focusing more on the richness and balance of sweet and savory notes rather than intense heat. However, the spice level is easily adjustable to your preference.

Mastering Butter Chicken is a rewarding culinary journey that brings the vibrant flavors of India right to your dining table. With this guide, you're well-equipped to create a dish that will impress and delight, solidifying its place as a cherished favorite in your recipe repertoire.
